steps.title: Find the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: The line of best fit crosses the y-axis at $x = 0$, where $y = 13.5$. Since the y-intercept is positive, this eliminates choices C and D, which both have $-13.5$ as the constant term.

steps.title: Determine the slope
steps.content:
The line descends from left to right, so the slope is negative. This immediately eliminates choice A, which has a slope of $+0.8$. To find the exact slope, identify two points on the line: $(0, 13.5)$ and $(10, 5.5)$.
$$\text{slope} = \frac{5.5 - 13.5}{10 - 0} = \frac{-8}{10} = -0.8$$

steps.title: Write the equation and identify the choice
steps.content: With a y-intercept of $13.5$ and a slope of $-0.8$, the equation is $y = 13.5 - 0.8x$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
